questions.question_text: What is the name of the Kentucky seller disclosure form?
distractors: KREC Form 400
KREC Form 203
KREC Form 500
questions.explanation: KREC Form 402 is the Seller's Disclosure of Property Condition form. Form 400 is the agency disclosure ('A Guide to Agency Relationships').

questions.question_text: A Kentucky home is offered for sale by owner without a listing agreement, and a licensee is involved in the transaction. After the owner completes the disclosure form, within how many hours of the creation of the executory contract must the licensee deliver it to the buyer?
distractors: 48 hours (2 days)
72 hours (3 days)
240 hours (10 days)
questions.explanation: When property is sold without a listing agreement and a licensee is involved, KRS 324.360(5) requires the licensee to deliver the completed Seller's Disclosure of Property Condition form to the buyer within 120 hours (5 days) after creation of any executory contract. For LISTED property, the rule is different: the listing agent must deliver the form within 72 hours of receiving a written and signed offer to purchase (KRS 324.360(4)).

questions.question_text: A home is being sold FSBO with a licensee involved, so the 120-hour delivery rule applies. The purchase contract is created on Monday at 2 PM. By when must the licensee deliver the seller disclosure form to the buyer?
distractors: By Wednesday at 2 PM
By Thursday at 2 PM
By the following Monday at 2 PM
questions.explanation: Under KRS 324.360(5), in a sale without a listing agreement the licensee must deliver the disclosure form within 120 hours (5 days) of creation of the executory contract. Counting from Monday at 2 PM, the deadline is Saturday at 2 PM. (For listed property, the listing agent instead has 72 hours from receipt of a written, signed offer — KRS 324.360(4).)

questions.question_text: Which type of property triggers the Kentucky seller disclosure requirement?
distractors: All residential and commercial properties
Properties valued over $100,000 only
Multi-family properties with 4+ units
questions.explanation: KRS 324.360(1) applies to sales of single-family residential dwellings when a licensee receives compensation. Commercial property is not covered, and by regulation (201 KAR 11:121 Section 4) licensees must also direct seller-clients of duplexes, triplexes, fourplexes, condominiums, and townhouses to complete the form.

questions.question_text: A condition changes after the disclosure form is completed but before closing. What must the seller do?
questions.correct_answer: Notify the agent or buyer in writing
distractors: Nothing is required until after closing
Complete an entirely new disclosure form
Cancel the existing purchase contract
questions.explanation: Sellers must notify the agent or buyer in writing of any changes to the disclosed conditions before closing. This ensures the buyer has current information.

questions.question_text: Which transaction is EXEMPT from Kentucky's seller disclosure form requirement?
distractors: A FSBO sale in which a licensee receives compensation
The sale of a 30-year-old home through a listing agent
The sale of an inherited home listed with a broker
questions.explanation: KRS 324.360(7) exempts only three transactions: residential purchases of new homes if a warranty is offered, sales of real estate at auction, and court-supervised foreclosures. Estate/inherited-home sales and FSBO sales involving a compensated licensee are not exempt.

questions.question_text: Which of the following must be disclosed on the Kentucky seller disclosure form?
distractors: That a previous owner had HIV
That a murder occurred on the property
The seller's original purchase price
questions.explanation: Methamphetamine contamination is a required disclosure on KREC Form 402 (see KRS 224.1-410(10)). By contrast, a prior occupant's HIV status or a homicide on the property is not a required disclosure — under KRS 324.162 a licensee has no affirmative duty to disclose information not required by KRS Chapter 324 or federal law, and federal fair housing law protects against HIV/AIDS-related disclosure. A seller's original purchase price is not a required disclosure.

questions.question_text: A seller knows radon testing was performed and showed elevated levels. Must this be disclosed?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, radon results must be disclosed
distractors: Only if levels exceed EPA guidelines
No, radon is not on the KY form
Only in certain Kentucky counties
questions.explanation: Radon testing and mitigation is one of the required disclosure categories on KREC Form 402. Sellers must disclose known radon test results.

questions.question_text: Which environmental hazard disclosure items appear on the Kentucky form?
distractors: Only lead-based paint hazard disclosures
Only asbestos and mold issue disclosures
Only radon and meth contamination items
questions.explanation: The Kentucky disclosure form includes environmental hazards such as carbon monoxide, asbestos, and urea formaldehyde insulation, along with mold, radon, and meth contamination.

questions.question_text: A seller is aware of a roof leak that was temporarily patched. Must they disclose this?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, roof condition must be disclosed
distractors: No, if the patch stopped the leak
Only if the leak caused interior damage
Only for roofs over 10 years old
questions.explanation: Roof condition is a required disclosure section on KREC Form 402, which asks whether the roof has ever leaked — even before the seller owned the home — and whether repairs were made. A known leak, even if temporarily patched, must be disclosed.

questions.question_text: A seller refuses to complete the property condition disclosure form. What must the licensee do?
questions.correct_answer: Disclose the refusal to the buyer
distractors: Cancel the listing agreement
Complete the form for the seller
Report the seller to KREC
questions.explanation: If a seller refuses to complete Form 402, the refusal must be communicated in writing to the purchaser or prospective purchaser without unreasonable delay (KRS 324.360(8)), and the refusal is documented on the form. The licensee cannot complete the form for the seller unless the owner requests it in writing (KRS 324.360(9)).

questions.question_text: A seller completes Form 402 but later discovers a new issue with the HVAC system before closing. What is required?
distractors: Nothing, the form was already completed
A complete new Form 402
Verbal notification to the buyer's agent
questions.explanation: The seller must notify the agent or buyer in writing of any changes to the disclosed conditions before closing. A completely new form is not required — just written notice of the change.

questions.question_text: A buyer receives the seller disclosure 8 days after contract creation. What are the buyer's options?
distractors: This is within the normal timeframe
The contract is automatically void
The buyer must accept the late disclosure
questions.explanation: The delivery deadline (120 hours after contract creation in a no-listing-agreement sale; 72 hours after receipt of a signed offer for listed property) was missed. KRS 324.360 provides no automatic rescission right; late delivery is a license-law violation that can subject the licensee to KREC discipline, and any cancellation or renegotiation rights depend on the contract terms.

questions.question_text: A listing agent knows of a basement issue but the seller's disclosure form says 'unknown.' What should the agent do?
questions.correct_answer: Disclose the known issue to the buyer
distractors: Accept the seller's disclosure as final
Update the form without seller consent
Report the seller to KREC immediately
questions.explanation: Licensees have an independent duty to disclose known material facts. If the agent knows about an issue the seller marked as unknown, the agent must still disclose it to the buyer.

questions.question_text: The seller's disclosure form reveals previous mold remediation. Can the buyer use this to cancel the contract?
distractors: Yes, mold always allows cancellation
No, remediated mold is not material
Only if the mold returns before closing
questions.explanation: Whether mold remediation allows cancellation depends on the contract terms and contingencies. The disclosure informs the buyer, but cancellation rights depend on the specific agreement.
